title: aira alpina subsp. littoralis
intro: Aira alpina subsp. littoralis, also known as alpine hair grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the traditional folk medicine of the Alps. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for wound healing and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Aira alpina subsp. littoralis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal remedies for treating various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
